editableNewDialogue.text: That CW4Kids wiki page was fun. Could you also write a short “Programming Notes” section like Wikipedia has, explaining reruns, edits, and why some shows shuffle time slots?

## Programming notes

CW4Kids is scheduled as a Saturday-morning block, and its lineup is subject to periodic adjustments based on season rollovers, episode availability, and network standards practices.

### Reruns and rotation
Programming is frequently rotated in short “packages” of episodes, resulting in repeat airings during holiday weekends, sports preemptions, or between acquisition cycles. Multi-week marathons are occasionally used to bridge gaps between season blocks.

### Edits and broadcast versions
Some series air in edited broadcast versions to meet time-slot requirements (including commercial timing) and content guidelines. Edits may include shortened openings/endings, trimmed scenes, or replacement music cues depending on the materials supplied by licensors.

### Time-slot shuffles
Time slots may change when:
- a series transitions between story arcs or seasons;
- a new title premieres and requires a sampling window;
- certain episodes are held back to align with promotions;
- the block needs to maintain a genre mix (action/comedy) across the morning.

### Rights and episode availability
In some cases, only specific seasons of a franchise are licensed for broadcast, which can lead to continuous cycling of the available episodes rather than chronological expansion into later series.

### Preemptions
Local affiliates may preempt or reschedule the block for breaking news, special programming, or local commitments. When this occurs, CW4Kids typically resumes the planned rotation the following week.
Nice. Could you add one more bullet about how they sometimes pair “themed” episodes back-to-back across different shows?

Sure—here’s an added bullet you can drop into the “Time-slot shuffles” list:

- episodes are paired into occasional “themed” half-hours (e.g., sports, music, Halloween) across different series for promotional stunts and smoother audience flow.
If we’re doing another CW4Kids-style promo tweet, can you keep the same tongue-in-cheek vibe as that one where I had you say “sorry, Misty and Serena fans”?
Absolutely—I'll match that playful, gently apologetic tone (the kind of wink-nod that still keeps it friendly to the fans who feel left out).
Quick correction: I’m not really into strict renaming-convention stuff. That “Wally → Wolliriki” thing was just for that one GoGoRiki rewrite.

Got it—so it wasn’t a standing preference, just a one-off constraint for that specific prompt. When you want a GoGoRiki-style rewrite again, I’ll only do the character-name swaps if you explicitly ask for them.
Yeah, exactly—don’t assume I always want the names changed unless I say so.
